title: Elevated Planter Box
summary: A raised garden bed on 4x4 legs, built from untreated 2x6 boards so nothing but wood touches the soil, joined with metal strap-ties instead of internal joinery.
description:
Leaving the box itself untreated is a deliberate trade, not an oversight: treated lumber's preservatives are exactly what you don't want leaching into soil that's growing food, so only the legs — which never touch the growing medium — get the benefit of rot-resistant stock. Raising the whole bed off the ground on those legs also does double duty against a real pest problem: soil-dwelling insects like vine borer beetles have to actually climb up and over the legs to reach the plants, instead of simply being in the ground the bed sits on.

Every corner of this box is a metal strap-tie rather than a screwed butt joint or a mitered corner, which is what lets it go together fast with only a drill — no dado joints, no pocket holes, nothing that needs a jig. The trade is that the ties are visible on the outside of the box, which is a fair price for a raised bed that goes together in an afternoon.

materials.name: Pine, 2x6, 16 ft
materials.note: Untreated, so nothing leaches into the soil.

materials.name: Cedar, 4x4, 8 ft
materials.note: Rot-resistant — used only for the legs, which stay clear of the soil.

steps.title: Build the L-shaped wall corners
steps.body: Drill two screw holes on one end of a 60" side piece, spaced 3/4" from the end and 1-1/2" from the sides. Set a 19" end piece at a 90° angle, lining up the ends, and drive a 2" screw into each hole to form an L-shape. Repeat for all three pairs, so each of the three walls gets one L-shaped corner.

steps.title: Close each wall into a box
steps.body: Connect each L-shaped corner to its matching side and end piece, drilling two more holes and driving two more screws to close the L into a full box. You'll end up with three 22" x 60" wall boxes.

steps.title: Build the base and attach the legs
steps.body: On one of the three wall boxes, line up four 60" base boards side by side and drill two holes through each into the box wall below, fastening the solid base in place. Flip the box over so the base faces up, and attach a 4x4 leg at each corner: drill through the base into the leg and drive one screw, then reinforce with two bent strap-ties per leg (one on each side), fastened with screws.

steps.title: Stack and strap the remaining walls
steps.body: Flip the base-and-legs unit upright. Run a bead of wood glue along the top of the base wall, set the two remaining wall boxes in place, and join each seam with straight strap-ties — eight per wall, near each corner — fastened with screws on both ends.

images.alt: Elevated wooden planter box on four legs
